steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three places where mosaics are found: libraries, train stations, and public squares. The blank falls between "libraries" and "train stations," so it must supply the correct punctuation to separate items in a list.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "libraries: train": A colon introduces an explanation or definition, not a list item. Using a colon here breaks the list incorrectly. ✗
  • "libraries train": No punctuation between "libraries" and "train" makes the two words run together as if they form a single unit. ✗
  • "libraries—train": A dash signals an interruption or aside, not a standard separator between list items. ✗
  • "libraries, train": A comma correctly separates the first item from the second in a three-item list (libraries, train stations, and public squares). ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
